qualifying_org_definition: Any entity where the ABAWD performs unpaid work. Delaware Social Services Manual (DSSM) §9018.2 defines qualifying work as (A) work in exchange for money, (B) work in exchange for goods or services (in-kind work), and (C) unpaid work which is verified — with no requirement that the organization be pre-approved, registered, or have an MOU with DSS. No state-maintained approved volunteer site list was found in any primary source (E&T State Plan, Administrative Code, or DSSM).
verification_instrument: No Delaware-specific named form for volunteer-work verification was found in any primary source. DSSM §9018.2 states 'Unpaid work which is verified' qualifies. DSSM §9038 requires DSS to verify work hours at recertification. Per DSSM §9034–9035, documentary evidence is the primary verification method; acceptable sources include a written statement or letter from the organization confirming the volunteer's hours. Collateral contact (phone call to the organization) is also permitted. The household bears primary responsibility for providing verification.
hours_formula_detail: Delaware SNAP Alert and DSSM §9018.2 both explicitly state 80 hours per month (20 hours per week) as the ABAWD work requirement. Federal baseline applies; no workfare-formula variation found.
accepts_outofstate_detail: DSSM §9018.2 defines qualifying unpaid work as 'unpaid work which is verified' with no requirement that the organization be in-state, pre-approved, or registered with Delaware DSS. The policy is silent on org location. No source found restricting qualifying volunteer organizations to Delaware-incorporated or Delaware-registered entities. Under federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any qualifying nonprofit regardless of state of incorporation satisfies the requirement absent an explicit state restriction.
geographic_waivers: Delaware previously held ABAWD waivers for Wilmington City, Kent County, and Sussex County through September 30, 2025 (statewide waiver as of Q2 FY2024 per FNS records). The OBBBA (July 2025) tightened waiver criteria to require 10% unemployment; Delaware's prior waivers were not renewed under those terms. As of November 2025, Delaware enforces ABAWD requirements statewide with no active area waivers. State is fully enforced statewide as of 2026.
submission_deadline: Recipient must contact their case manager to prove compliance each month. If hours drop below 80/month, must notify case manager within 10 days. Formal verification of work hours is required at recertification per DSSM §9038. No specific paper deadline (e.g., 'by the 10th') was found in any public source.
notes:
Delaware's public SNAP guidance explicitly states volunteer work counts toward the 80-hour ABAWD monthly requirement, and the verification model appears to be recipient self-report to a DSS case manager with supporting documentation — no state-specific verification form name or number was found in any published Delaware source, and no mandatory prestep (EDP, county assignment, provider MOU) was identified. Confidence is LOW because Delaware has not published a detailed ABAWD volunteer verification policy manual or form publicly; the state may impose caseworker-discretion requirements that are not visible in public-facing web guidance.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Conducted adversarial adversarial research attempting to refute the Tier 1 classification. Searched for: Delaware SNAP E&T State Plan FY2026/FY2027 volunteer requirements; ABAWD work registration enrollment and case manager prestep; approved volunteer site lists and MOU requirements; verification forms and documentation. Key findings: (1) No pre-approved volunteer site list found — no Delaware-specific evidence of state-maintained org approval list or MOU gating for the general volunteer lane. (2) No mandatory EDP or work registration prestep found — SNAP E&T participation is explicitly voluntary per the FY2025 State Plan ('DSS encourages work registrants to volunteer with E&T'; referral is generated only 'if a participant agrees to volunteer with E&T'). (3) Volunteer work is confirmed by multiple sources to count toward the 80-hour requirement: the FY2025 State Plan and third-party aggregators (Propel, SNAP screener) all cite 'for pay, for goods or services, unpaid, or as a volunteer' language. (4) The primary state SNAP Alert page (dhss.delaware.gov/dss/snapalert/) does NOT explicitly list volunteer work — it only names paid employment and E&T participation — which is a communication inconsistency but not a policy restriction; the underlying FY2025 State Plan and other state guidance confirm volunteer counts. (5) Verification_instrument updated from 'unknown' to descriptive value: self-report to case manager with written documentation from the organization; no Delaware-specific form number is publicly named. (6) FY2026/FY2027 state plans are not yet publicly indexed; the FY2025 plan PDF returned 404 when fetched directly but its contents were partially retrieved via search engine indexing. Tier 1 classification survives adversarial check. Confidence raised from 'low' to 'medium' — multi-source confirmation that volunteer work counts and no mandatory prestep exists, but the absence of a published verification form and the snapalert page omission of volunteer work create enough ambiguity to prevent 'high'.

FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Successfully downloaded and extracted the full FY2025 Delaware SNAP E&T State Plan PDF (608KB, 91 pages) via the Wayback Machine snapshot at web.archive.org/web/20250830082039. Also extracted the full Delaware Social Services Manual chapter 9000 (Food Stamp Program) from its 2016 Wayback Machine snapshot, which contains the operational ABAWD policy at DSSM §9018.2. Key findings from primary PDF extraction: (1) DSSM §9018.2 ('Maintaining and Regaining ABAWD Eligibility', 7 CFR 273.24) defines 'work' explicitly as: A. Work in exchange for money; B. Work in exchange for goods or services (in-kind work); C. Unpaid work which is verified; D. Any combination of the above. This is the state's own definition in its operational policy manual — volunteer work (unpaid work) is a qualifying activity with no org pre-approval requirement. (2) The same section states that to regain eligibility, an ABAWD must 'work (paid or non-paid) for 80 hours or more' — again confirming unpaid/volunteer work counts with no organizational gating. (3) DSSM §9038 explicitly lists 'Work hours for individuals who are satisfying the ABAWD work requirements' as a mandatory verification item at recertification. (4) DSSM §9034-9035 define verification standards: documentary evidence is primary; collateral contacts (including phone calls to an employer/organization) are acceptable; households bear primary responsibility. No named form for ABAWD work-hour documentation exists. (5) The Delaware Administrative Code Title 16 Chapter 10000 (SNAP E&T) confirms the E&T program is fully voluntary ('Voluntary per 7 CFR 273.7(e)(5)(i)') and the state answered 'No' to enabling ABAWDs to regain eligibility through E&T within 30 days — confirming the general work/volunteer lane is the primary compliance path, not E&T. (6) Adversarial check: no pre-approved org list, no MOU requirement, no EDP prestep found in any primary source. The SNAP Alert page's omission of 'volunteer' from its qualifying activity list is a communications gap, not a policy restriction — the DSSM and E&T plan both use the broader federal definition. Confidence raised to HIGH: the primary policy manual (DSSM §9018.2) was directly read and unambiguously confirms the general volunteer lane with unpaid work counting; the E&T plan confirms the voluntary E&T program imposes no mandatory prestep; and adversarial search found no hidden blocking conditions. remote_stance updated to 'silent' (no primary source addresses whether remote/virtual volunteering qualifies).

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Added five new dimension groups. (1) Hours formula: federal_80 confirmed — Delaware SNAP Alert and DSSM §9018.2 both explicitly cite 80 hours/month; no workfare-formula variation found. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: 'silent' — DSSM §9018.2 imposes no org location, registration, or approval requirement; policy does not address org state of incorporation; federal default (any qualifying nonprofit) applies. (3) Geographic waivers: Delaware previously held waivers for Wilmington City, Kent County, and Sussex County through Sept 30, 2025 (statewide as of FY2024). OBBBA tightened waiver criteria to 10% unemployment threshold; Delaware's waivers were not renewed. Statewide enforcement confirmed as of November 2025. (4) Submission UX: primary mechanism is phone to case manager (1-866-843-7212) or in-person at State Service Center; no online portal or specific paper form publicly named; reporting is monthly; 10-day notification required if hours drop below threshold; formal verification at recertification per DSSM §9038. (5) Form fields: no named Delaware verification form; defaulting to reasonable baseline fields for an org verification letter consistent with DSSM documentary evidence standards.
